title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure could lead to significant data loss, service disruptions, and financial damage. Compromising CI/CD pipelines may also result in the deployment of malicious code across multiple environments, amplifying the impact.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unusual activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and mitigate ransomware threats in real-time.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Event Log detail endpoint `GET /api/v2/eventLogs/{event_log_id}` in Apache Airflow fetched...
description: The Event Log detail endpoint `GET /api/v2/eventLogs/{event_log_id}` in Apache Airflow fetched audit-log rows directly by numeric ID after only the generic Audit Log permission check, while the collection endpoint `GET /api/v2/eventLogs` applied per-Dag scoping. An authenticated UI/API user with audit-log read permission for one Dag could retrieve audit-log entries for any other Dag by guessing or enumerating the numeric event log ID. Affects deployments that rely on per-Dag audit-log scoping. U

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46764.

title: Incomplete authorization by Apache ActiveMQ server before versions v6.2.6 and v5.19.7 allows...
description:
Incomplete authorization by Apache ActiveMQ server before versions v6.2.6 and v5.19.7 allows authenticated connections to remove existing destinations with proper permissions.

This issue affects Apache ActiveMQ Broker: before 5.19.7, from 6.0.0 before 6.2.6; Apache ActiveMQ All: before 5.19.7, from 6.0.0 before 6.2.6; Apache ActiveMQ: before 5.19.7, from 6.0.0 before 6.2.6.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version v6.2.6 or v5.19.7, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46605.

title: Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Metadata vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Broker,...
description:
Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Metadata vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Broker, Apache ActiveMQ, Apache ActiveMQ All.

Brokers that are configured with a network connector with syncDurableSubs set to true, are vulnerable to an unauthenticated attacker who can receive a list of all durable topic subscriptions in the broker, including client identifiers, subscription names, topic destinations, and JMS selector expressions, by sending a BrokerInfo command. The broker incorrectly respond

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive metadata about durable topic subscriptions in Apache ActiveMQ brokers. This could lead to information disclosure, enabling attackers to map the broker's communication structure and potentially exploit other vulnerabilities or launch targeted attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and disable the 'syncDurableSubs' setting in network connectors if not explicitly required.
Apply any available patches or updates from Apache addressing CVE-2026-49270.
Monitor broker logs for suspicious BrokerInfo commands and implement network-level access controls to restrict unauthenticated access.

title: A Deserialization of Untrusted Data vulnerability affecting Teamwork Cloud from No Magic Release...
description: A Deserialization of Untrusted Data vulnerability affecting Teamwork Cloud from No Magic Release 2022x through No Magic Release 2026x and Magic Collaboration Studio from CATIA Magic Release 2022x through CATIA Magic Release 2026x could lead to an unauthenticated remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The Deserialization of Untrusted Data vulnerability (CVE-2026-7858) in Teamwork Cloud and Magic Collaboration Studio allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely. This could lead to full system compromise, data breaches, or disruption of critical operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply available patches or updates provided by the vendor to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation and access controls to limit exposure of affected systems to untrusted networks.
Monitor systems for any unusual activity or signs of exploitation, and ensure incident response plans are ready to be executed.

title: Apache Airflow's EmailOperator and the underlying `airflow.utils.email` helpers established SMTP...
description: Apache Airflow's EmailOperator and the underlying `airflow.utils.email` helpers established SMTP STARTTLS connections without verifying the remote certificate when the deployment used `[email] smtp_starttls=True` without `[email] smtp_ssl`. An attacker positioned between the worker and the configured SMTP server (network MITM — typical hostile-network attack-surface for environments where the SMTP relay sits outside the worker's trust boundary) could present a self-signed certificate, have the w

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49267.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's KubernetesExecutor caused JWT tokens used by worker pods to...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's KubernetesExecutor caused JWT tokens used by worker pods to authenticate against the Execution API to be passed to the worker container as command-line arguments visible in the pod spec. An authenticated UI/API user with Kubernetes read-only access to the cluster (e.g. `pods/get` in the Airflow namespace) could harvest the JWT from `kubectl describe pod` output and then call state-mutating Execution API endpoints — triggering Dag runs, clearing runs, reading or writing

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The bug in Apache Airflow's KubernetesExecutor exposes sensitive JWT tokens, allowing unauthorized users with Kubernetes read-only access to manipulate DAG runs and potentially compromise the integrity of workflows. This could lead to unauthorized data access, workflow disruptions, or malicious execution within affected systems.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of Apache Airflow that addresses CVE-2026-49298.
Restrict Kubernetes namespace permissions to prevent unauthorized access to pod specifications.
Audit Kubernetes clusters for any signs of token harvesting or unauthorized API usage.

title:
Incorrect Default Permissions vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ.

This issue affects Apache...
description:
Incorrect Default Permissions vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ.

This issue affects Apache ActiveMQ: before 5.19.7, from 6.0.0 before 6.2.6.

The default Jolokia authorization settings granted non-admin (low-privilege) web-login accounts access to Jolokia operations which allowed executing broker management operations meant for admins such as addQueue and removeQueue.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 6.2.6 or 5.19.7, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49157.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's auth manager logout handling left previously-issued JWT tokens valid...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's auth manager logout handling left previously-issued JWT tokens valid after the user clicked logout in the UI: the logout flow for `FabAuthManager` and `KeycloakAuthManager` did not actually reach the underlying `revoke_token()` call, so the JWT remained accepted by the API server until its natural expiry. An attacker holding a previously-issued JWT for a logged-out user could continue to make authenticated API calls as that user. Affects deployments configured with `Fab

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-48726.

title: Path traversal vulnerability in Apache MINA SSHD bundle sshd-git. Lack of path validation in git...
description:
Path traversal vulnerability in Apache MINA SSHD bundle sshd-git. Lack of path validation in git-upload-pack, git-receive-pack, and other git operations allows users authenticated over SSH access to git repositories outside the configured git server root directory.




Applications are affected if they use org.apache.sshd:sshd-git. Applications not using sshd-git are not affected.




Users are advised to upgrade affected applications to Apche MINA SSHD 2.18.0, which fixes the issue.




The iss

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The path traversal vulnerability in Apache MINA SSHD's sshd-git module allows authenticated users to access files and directories outside the intended git server root directory. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, potential data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the host system.
recommended_actions: Immediately identify and inventory all applications using org.apache.sshd:sshd-git and determine if they are running a vulnerable version.
Upgrade to Apache MINA SSHD version 2.18.0 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict SSH access to trusted users and monitor SSH activity for any unusual or unauthorized behavior.

title: Apache Fluss versions prior to 0.9.1 configure the Netty LengthFieldBasedFrameDecoder with...
description:
Apache Fluss versions prior to 0.9.1 configure the Netty LengthFieldBasedFrameDecoder with Integer.MAX_VALUE as the maximum frame length, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to exhaust JVM heap memory on TabletServer and CoordinatorServer by sending specially crafted frame headers, resulting in denial of service.

This issue affects Apache Fluss (incubating): 0.8.0 and 0.9.0.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 0.9.1, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49361.

title:
A vulnerability was discovered on Stormshield Network Security 





  *  4.3.0 to 4.3.41, 
  * ...
description:
A vulnerability was discovered on Stormshield Network Security 





  *  4.3.0 to 4.3.41, 
  *  4.8.0 to 4.8.15, 
  *  5.0.0 to 5.0.5








It is possible to execute a reflected XSS attack on the login API available on Stormshield SNS appliance by executing a script on the victim's machine. The risks include the theft of cookies or other sensitive data, as well as the modification of page behavior, for example, by redirecting the victim to malicious websites.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute a reflected XSS attack on the login API of Stormshield SNS appliances. This can lead to the theft of sensitive data, such as cookies, or manipulation of the victim's browser behavior, potentially redirecting them to malicious websites.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches provided by Stormshield to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement input sanitization and output encoding on the affected login API to prevent XSS attacks.
Educate users on recognizing phishing attempts and avoiding clicking on suspicious links that could exploit this vulnerability.

title: A Stored Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability affecting Process Experience Studio in DELMIA...
description: A Stored Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability affecting Process Experience Studio in DELMIA Service Process Engineer from Release 3DEXPERIENCE R2024x through Release 3DEXPERIENCE R2026x could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary script code in user's browser session.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9024.

title: Improper Input Validation, Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')...
description:
Improper Input Validation, Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Broker, Apache ActiveMQ All, Apache ActiveMQ.


Non-parenthesized discovery wrappers such as `masterslave:vm://...,...`
and `static:vm://...` incorrectly pass validation allowing bypass of fix in CVE-2026-34197. 

Original description from CVE-2026-34197.

Apache ActiveMQ exposes the Jolokia JMX-HTTP bridge at /api/jolokia/ on the web console. The default Jolokia access policy pe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45505.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's rendered-template field handling caused nested sensitive-key masking (e...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's rendered-template field handling caused nested sensitive-key masking (e.g. nested `password` / `token` / `secret` / `api_key` keys inside a JSON template structure) to be bypassed when the rendered field exceeded `[core] max_templated_field_length`: Airflow stringified the structure before redaction, losing the nested key context, and persisted the plaintext value into `rendered_fields`. An authenticated UI/API user with permission to read rendered template fields could

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Apache Airflow's handling of rendered-template fields allows sensitive information, such as passwords, tokens, secrets, and API keys, to be exposed in plaintext within the 'rendered_fields' when the field length exceeds a certain threshold. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data by authenticated users with appropriate permissions, potentially resulting in data breaches or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest patched version of Apache Airflow that addresses CVE-2026-42360 to ensure proper handling and redaction of sensitive keys.
Restrict access to the Airflow UI/API by limiting permissions to only trusted and essential users.
Audit and monitor access logs for any unauthorized attempts to read 'rendered_fields' and review configurations for sensitive data exposure.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's bulk Task Instances API (`PATCH/DELETE /api/v2/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns/...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's bulk Task Instances API (`PATCH/DELETE /api/v2/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns/{dag_run_id}/taskInstances`) evaluated authorization against the `dag_id` resolved from the URL path while operating on the `dag_id` / `dag_run_id` extracted from request-body entity fields. An authenticated UI/API user with edit permission on one Dag could mutate Task Instance state in any other Dag by keeping the authorized Dag's ID in the URL path and naming the target Dag's IDs in the request body

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41084.

title: Improper Input Validation, Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')...
description:
Improper Input Validation, Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Broker, Apache ActiveMQ All, Apache ActiveMQ.

Apache ActiveMQ Classic exposes the Jolokia JMX-HTTP bridge at /api/jolokia/ on the web console. The default Jolokia access policy permits exec operations on all ActiveMQ MBeans (org.apache.activemq:*), including
BrokerService.addNetworkConnector(String).

An authenticated attacker can invoke these operations with a crafted discovery

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code on Apache ActiveMQ instances by exploiting the Jolokia JMX-HTTP bridge. This could lead to unauthorized network connections, data exfiltration, or complete system compromise, depending on the attacker's objectives.
recommended_actions: Restrict access to the Jolokia JMX-HTTP bridge by updating the Jolokia access policy to limit exec operations or disable it entirely if not required.
Apply the latest security patches or updates for Apache ActiveMQ as soon as they are released to address CVE-2026-42588.
Implement network segmentation and firewall rules to restrict access to the ActiveMQ management interface to trusted IP addresses only.

title: Exploitation requires the attacker to already be an authenticated Airflow worker holding a valid...
description: Exploitation requires the attacker to already be an authenticated Airflow worker holding a valid Log-server JWT issued for at least one Dag. Apache Airflow's Log server authorized JWT tokens against Dag IDs by applying Python's `str.lstrip()` to the requested path segment when verifying the JWT's `sub` claim. `str.lstrip()` strips any of a *set* of characters from the left (not a prefix), so a JWT issued for a Dag named e.g. `dag_a` would authorize log access to any other Dag whose name began wi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45426.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's XCom PATCH endpoint `PATCH /api/v2/xcomEntries/{key}` allowed an...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's XCom PATCH endpoint `PATCH /api/v2/xcomEntries/{key}` allowed an authenticated UI/API user with XCom write permission on a Dag to set XCom entries under reserved key names (e.g. `return_value`) that the matching POST endpoint already validated against `FORBIDDEN_XCOM_KEYS`. The endpoint also accepted serialized payload shapes the triggerer's deserializer treats as code; combined, this allowed RCE on the triggerer when the affected task next deferred. Affects deployments

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42359.

title: Apache Airflow's scheduler-side deadline-reference decoder (`SerializedCustomReference...
description: Apache Airflow's scheduler-side deadline-reference decoder (`SerializedCustomReference.deserialize_reference`) imported and dispatched arbitrary class paths drawn from DAG-author-controlled serialized state without an allowlist or plugin-registry gate. A DAG author whose code reaches the scheduler — the default on single-host deployments where the DAG bundle is importable from the scheduler process — could embed a custom `DeadlineReference` whose serialized form named an attacker-controlled modu

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a malicious DAG author to execute arbitrary code on the Apache Airflow scheduler by embedding a custom `DeadlineReference` in serialized DAGs. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or disruption of critical workflows.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Apache Airflow that addresses CVE-2026-45360 as soon as it becomes available.
Implement strict access controls to limit who can upload or modify DAGs, especially in single-host deployments.
Enable runtime monitoring and logging to detect and respond to suspicious activity in the scheduler process.

title: A bug in Apache Airflow's Variable response masker caused nested-key redaction (triggered by...
description: A bug in Apache Airflow's Variable response masker caused nested-key redaction (triggered by secret-suffixed key names like `password`, `token`, `secret`, `api_key`) to be bypassed when the JSON value's nesting depth exceeded the shared secrets masker's recursion limit: the masker returned the original nested item before checking the sensitive key name. An authenticated UI/API user with Variable read permission could harvest plaintext secret values stored under sensitive keys nested deep enough

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42358.

title: CVE-2026-8652 - An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrat
description: An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrator access to the product’s web console, they may be able to execute arbitrary OS commands via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker with administrator access to execute arbitrary OS commands, potentially leading to system compromise, data exfiltration, or further lateral movement within the network. This poses a significant risk to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict access to the web console by implementing IP whitelisting or VPN access to limit exposure.
Apply available patches or firmware updates provided by the vendor to address the vulnerability.
Regularly monitor and audit administrator account activity for any unauthorized or suspicious actions.

title: CVE-2026-6059 - A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web b
description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web browser of a user accessing the web management interface via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6059.

title: CVE-2026-9489 - NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program
description: NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing any authenticated local user to execute arbitrary code with NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM privileges and to delete arbitrary files with SYSTEM privileges. By leveraging this, an attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target system with elevated privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9489.

title: CVE-2026-9410 - A vulnerability has been found in Sushmi-pal Invoice-System up to a0a3faa16dee2621b231ae227333f57616
description: A vulnerability has been found in Sushmi-pal Invoice-System up to a0a3faa16dee2621b231ae227333f5761607283b. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /profile of the component Profile Workflow. Such manipulation of the argument ID leads to improper authorization. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. This product takes the approach of rolling releases to provide continious delivery. Therefore, version details for affected and updated releases are not available. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: CVE-2026-9408 - A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A8000RU 7.1cu.643_b20200521. Affected by this issue is the
description: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A8000RU 7.1cu.643_b20200521.
